layman_headline: Market fear and weak charts say patience pays — no new trades today.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with most altcoins showing bearish chart structures. Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.4%, meaning money is flowing out of smaller coins and into Bitcoin. While a few coins had big recent moves (PUMP up 28%, LDO up 13%), those are already extended and risky to chase. The best potential setup is Ethereum near oversold levels, but its chart is still bearish and support at $1,800 hasn't been tested yet — better to wait for a cleaner entry.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is scared and most coins are in downtrends. If Ethereum dips to $1,800 and holds that support with increasing volume, that could be a safer entry point in the next few days.
expert_reasoning: Every candidate has significant flaws. BONK has a -2.0 chart bias with Three Black Crows — catching a falling knife. PUMP and LDO are already extended after +28% and +13% weekly moves with RSIs approaching overbought territory. JTO and WLD have poor risk/reward ratios (0.52x and 0.38x). BinanceLife (币安人生) has a bull flag but the token is obscure with questionable liquidity. ETH at RSI 39 with $1,800 key support nearby offers the cleanest eventual setup, but the chart bias is still -0.70 with price below all major MAs. The CryptoSlate headline warning about $2.3B stablecoin liquidity drain exposing $57K BTC is a macro risk. High BTC dominance at 56.4% means alt positions carry extra risk. The prudent move is to wait for ETH to test and hold $1,800 or for fear/greed to drop further into extreme fear territory for a higher-conviction contrarian entry.
risk_factors: Stablecoin liquidity drain of $2.3B could trigger another leg down across all crypto assets
BTC dominance at 56.4% and rising means altcoin capital continues bleeding to Bitcoin
Allbridge exploit and Ethereum bridge shutdown news may further erode DeFi confidence
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a hold with volume confirmation would be an actionable entry signal
Russia crypto bill final readings Tuesday — regulatory clarity could move markets either direction
Fear & Greed index — if it drops below 20 (extreme fear) while ETH holds support, that's a high-conviction buy zone
market_context: Crypto markets are in a fear phase (29/100) with BTC struggling below $64,400 amid an oil bounce and AI selloff. BTC dominance at 56.4% signals capital rotation away from altcoins, making alt swing trades higher risk than usual.
context_banner: Fear-driven market with weak alt charts — discipline demands waiting for support confirmation before deploying capital.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold but hasn't crossed the 30 threshold yet — close but not compelling enough alone for entry.
Fear & Greed at 29 (Fear) and improving from 25.9 weekly average — contrarian setup building but not yet at extreme fear levels.
Warning about $2.3B stablecoin liquidity drain exposing BTC to $57K downside adds meaningful macro risk to any new position.
Grayscale planning ETH/SOL staking reward payouts and Russia crypto bill final readings provide upcoming catalysts worth monitoring.
All top candidates show either bearish chart structures (BONK -2.0, ETH -0.7) or are already extended (LDO +0.85 but RSI 59 and +13% in 7 days) — no clean setups available.
